phase_a.summary: All 6 ACs (R1-R6) traceable to test scenarios. 40/40 scenarios green.

dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.evidence: templates/reintegration.md.tmpl never consumed; generate-reintegration.sh:409-420 hardcodes markdown render.

dimensions.name: test-coverage
dimensions.evidence: Test checks template exists + has markers; never verifies generator reads it.

dimensions.name: spec-compliance
dimensions.evidence: Spec says update-summary reintegration exits 1; bin/rws:2282-2285 exits 3.

dimensions.name: unplanned-changes
dimensions.evidence: Diff includes wave-3 files (false positive: codex compares full base..HEAD).

evaluator_notes.rationale: AC-R5 says template is 'used when worktree completes without prior content'. Confirmed via ripgrep: generator hardcodes rendering. Template file exists but is never loaded. Either (a) wire generator to read from template, or (b) remove the template and update AC-R5 to match reality. The spec intent was (a).

The exit code discrepancy is real. Spec §AC-R1 reads 'validate-summary... exit 1 on failure' in places but 'update-summary... exit 3' elsewhere; codex may be reading the wrong section. Impl uses 3 for 'validation error' which matches project's standard exit-code convention (0/1/2/3/4). Worth confirming which the spec actually wants — my read is 3 is correct but codex found a contradicting passage.

Codex computed diff against base_commit (a284525), which includes ALL work across all waves and Furrow-managed artifacts. Actual deliverable-specific diff is within worktree-reintegration-summary's ownership. Evaluator artifact, not a violation.
SC2038 is 'Use -print0/-0 or -exec + to allow for non-alphanumeric filenames'. Trivial shellcheck fix. Not a bug; style warning.

overall_rationale: Template dead-code is a real AC-R5 gap. Exit-code discrepancy is minor. Bringing template into use would convert to PASS.

action_items.item: Wire generate-reintegration.sh to load markdown skeleton from templates/reintegration.md.tmpl instead of hardcoded render. Add a test scenario that modifies the template and asserts the change surfaces in the rendered output.
Resolve exit-code discrepancy with spec §AC-R1 (confirm exit 3 or change to exit 1).
